summary: No-ship: the committed RED proof is valid and exercises the exact pass-abort symptom, but Round 22's acceptance gate rejects valid integration witnesses and does not enforce zero ignored tests. Simpler alternative: use one target-aware discovery script as the registry authority instead of duplicating basename lists across shell and W-REG. Open question: none.

findings.title: Discovery anchor rejects valid integration witnesses
findings.body: Several required witnesses are top-level functions in integration-test crates, which libtest lists as `<id>: test`. The loop requires `::<id>: test`, so correctly implemented W4/W7/W9/W10c/W17 and W13 witnesses will be reported missing before the suites run.
findings.recommendation: Match both crate-root and nested names with `(^|::)<id>: test$`, or verify exact target-qualified harness names stored in the canonical table.

findings.title: The zero-ignored gate is only prose
findings.body: No executable acceptance command parses ignored counts or fails when a witness is ignored. Cargo therefore exits successfully for the exact `1 ignored` attack documented by the plan, leaving B-IGNORE open despite describing `0 ignored` as a gate.
findings.recommendation: Capture every suite result and exit nonzero on any nonzero ignored count. Also reject `#[ignore]` additions or deliberately execute ignored tests with `--include-ignored`.

next_steps: Correct the matcher and verify it against one nested lib witness and one crate-root integration witness.
Implement an executable zero-ignored assertion, then repeat the plan review without running GREEN tests.
